case.why_selected: Tests whether the schema can hold softness, editorial restraint, emotional language, and ritualized surface behavior without overclaiming strategy.

public_case_page.title: Charms x FLOC* - The Future of Agentic Games Onchain

public_case_copy_excerpts.text: The Future of Agentic Games Onchain
every game becomes a social ritual

local_prior_case_artifacts.use: visual observation anchor produced before this operational record
local summary of visible public page evidence

confidence_semantics.high: Directly stated in public copy, directly listed in public metadata, or anchored to existing local visual observations with direct visible evidence.
confidence_semantics.medium: Reasonable interpretation from public copy or from a single anchored visual observation, but not independently repeated.
confidence_semantics.low: Plausible but weak inference from aesthetics, tone, or incomplete evidence; requires human review before reuse.

extracted_facts.fact: The public case headline frames Charms as agentic games onchain.
The public case copy links AI, emotion, play, meaningful connection, and social ritual.
The public page identifies the work as a Design Sprint.
The public copy says the engagement produced brand strategy, visual direction, creative foundations, a Framer landing-page concept, and $CHARMS visual identity.

visual_observations.observation: The visible system is described locally as soft, light-based, and atmospheric rather than hard-interface driven.
visual_observations.downstream_note: Useful for Visual Signature as a surface-behavior anchor, but should be rechecked against screenshots before automation.

visual_observations.observation: Repeated rounded light forms, serif wordmarks, blue fields, and white negative space create a gentle recurring rhythm.
visual_observations.downstream_note: A reviewer should distinguish system repetition from decorative repetition.

visual_observations.observation: Hierarchy is described as scale plus silence: large serif typography carries the emotional center while navigation and support text recede.
visual_observations.downstream_note: Do not treat hierarchy only as readability; this record frames it as perceptual priority.

visual_observations.observation: The interaction layer is described as lookbook-like: gallery-first pacing, delayed CTA, and threshold-like exit behavior.
visual_observations.downstream_note: This needs direct browser/capture evidence before becoming a reusable interaction pattern.

strategic_interpretations.interpretation: Charms translates a technical onchain gaming category into a softer emotional ritual frame.
strategic_interpretations.why_not_high_confidence: The interpretation combines stated copy with visual reading; intent is plausible but not independently confirmed.

strategic_interpretations.interpretation: The case suggests that premium play can be expressed through restraint instead of speed, spectacle, or maximal game tropes.
strategic_interpretations.why_not_high_confidence: The public page does not state premium intent; this is a Brand3 reading of surface behavior.

unsupported_or_low_confidence_inferences.inference: The intended audience may be closer to design-literate Web3 players than to mainstream mobile gamers.
unsupported_or_low_confidence_inferences.why_low_confidence: Audience is not directly stated; the inference comes from aesthetic cues.

unsupported_or_low_confidence_inferences.inference: The case may be intentionally avoiding conventional gaming intensity.
unsupported_or_low_confidence_inferences.why_low_confidence: Avoidance is an intent claim; current evidence only supports the absence of hard-interface or aggressive gaming cues.

contradictions.stated_claim: The public copy frames the project as games onchain.
contradictions.observed_signal: The local visual observations describe calm editorial pacing, soft light forms, serif typography, and lookbook-like interaction rather than high-intensity game UI.
contradictions.interpretation_boundary: This is not a flaw claim. It records a productive tension between category language and surface behavior.

contradictions.stated_claim: The public copy says the brand is modular.
contradictions.observed_signal: Current visual evidence is stronger for repetition and motif control than for explicit modular component behavior.
contradictions.interpretation_boundary: Do not convert the modular claim into a confirmed system property without component or layout evidence.

review_questions: Do screenshots confirm the visual observations used as anchors?
Is the soft contradiction between onchain games and editorial pacing productive or misleading?
Should modularity remain a copy-level fact until component evidence is available?
Which terms are safe to reuse outside Charms without importing FLOC* case-study voice?
